twitter_thread: 62% of iPhone users have over 1,000 duplicate photos. The Apple Photos 'Clean Up' Tool can fix this
The 'Clean Up' Tool removes 30% of photos on average, mostly duplicates and screenshots
Back up your 5,000+ photos before using the 'Clean Up' Tool to avoid losing 10% of your library
Users with 10,000+ photos see the most benefit from the 'Clean Up' Tool, with 50% fewer duplicates
Combine the 'Clean Up' Tool with 'Albums' to organize your 500+ photos from last year's vacation
The 'Clean Up' Tool saved me 2 hours of manual photo sorting. Can it replace Adobe Lightroom?
What's your experience with the Apple Photos 'Clean Up' Tool? #photomanagement #applephotos
pack.linkedin: The Apple Photos 'Clean Up' Tool can eliminate 30% of your photos, mostly duplicates and screenshots. Before using it, back up your library to prevent losing 10% of your photos. This tool is particularly useful for users with large libraries, such as 10,000+ photos. For example, combining it with the 'Albums' feature can help organize your 500+ photos from last year's vacation. But can it replace third-party tools like Adobe Lightroom? What's your experience with the Apple Photos 'Clean Up' Tool?
tiktok_hooks: Got 1,000 duplicate photos on your iPhone?
This one iPhone feature can delete 30% of your photos
Sick of sorting photos manually? There's a better way
pack.reddit_headline: Can the Apple Photos 'Clean Up' Tool replace Adobe Lightroom for photo management?
